title: Surface sign
goal: Put readable text on the face of a Part using SurfaceGui.

steps.body:
A lot of Roblox worlds use in-world signage — quest markers, shop
labels, puzzle hints. The trick is `SurfaceGui`: it projects a
regular 2D UI onto a chosen face of a Part. We'll build one.

Make a tall, flat Part to act as the sign body. Anchored so physics
leaves it alone.

Add a SurfaceGui as a child of the Part. We set `Face = Front` so it
draws on the +Z face.
Drop a TextLabel inside the SurfaceGui, filling it, with the headline text.

Static signs are fine, but a gentle pulse reads much more alive.
Ask Assistant to animate the text.
steps.suggestedPrompt:
Add a Script under Workspace.SignPost.Display.Headline that uses
TweenService to pulse the TextLabel's TextTransparency between 0 and
0.4 with a 1.5-second period, looping forever.

steps.body:
Press ▶ **Play** (or F5). Walk around your SignPost — you should see
"WELCOME" rendered on the front face of the wooden board, readable
from any reasonable distance. Shift+F5 to stop.
